models.name: NES System
models.description: The hero console in the set. It is the right place to hang power states, sound cues, and lighting changes.

states.label: Power On
states.description: Idle state for the front-panel and bezel treatment.

states.label: Power off
states.description: Hook a power-on clip to this state when the GLB exports an animation with the matching clip name.

about.title: NES System overview
about.summary: A large, static-friendly hero model that can drive the most complete set of animation, sound, and state hooks.
details: Use this manifest entry when you want the front page or detail page to feel like a centerpiece rather than a compact accessory.
If the exported GLB includes a power animation clip, the on state can trigger it and keep the surrounding UI in sync.
This entry is also a good template for adding extra images or video references once you have renders or assembly footage.

models.name: Controller
models.description: A compact object with no required state machine. It is ideal for texture-led presentation and simple orbit inspection.

about.title: Controller reference
about.summary: This model stays intentionally simple so it can act as a clean comparison object without animation requirements.
details: Leave the state list empty when a model does not need any on/off, armed, or inserted behaviors.
This entry demonstrates that the manifest can still provide rich descriptive content without animations or sounds.
You can add preview media later if you want to show a render turntable or a close-up breakdown.

models.name: Cartridge
models.description: A small object that can expose an inserted state, but does not need animation to be useful.

about.title: Cartridge detail
about.summary: A state-light model that still benefits from a small amount of behavior and finish switching.
details: This is the clearest example of a model that changes state without any clip-based animation.
If you later add a label decal or shell color variant, the state can swap to the matching texture key.
Use the inserted state if the cartridge should visually read as installed rather than loose.

models.name: Zapper
models.description: A more action-oriented model where a button press can trigger a ready or armed state and optionally an animation clip.

about.title: Zapper reference
about.summary: A model that benefits from explicit state switching because it feels more alive when the user can arm it.
details: This entry is the best place to test a state change that does more than swap the finish.
If the exported GLB eventually contains a Raise or similar clip, the armed state can play it when pressed.
You can expand this section with reference renders, exploded views, or short usage clips later on.
